result:
All files are in place. Here is a summary of what was authored:

---

## Phase Brief — `phase-01-provider-switch`

### Objective

Close the gap where `ResolveProvider` returns `Vendor:"anthropic"` unconditionally — so that a TIDE operator can configure a Project whose planner levels run Claude and whose executor levels run Codex, and the controller delivers the correct `ProviderSpec.Vendor` to each dispatched Job without any Codex-specific code touching the controller or `cmd/manager`.

### Scope

Pure controller-side schema + resolver + Helm chart wiring. No Codex CLI, no container image, no usage/pricing code — those belong to later phases. The deliverable is the **infrastructure that makes per-level vendor selection work** from day one of the Codex subagent's existence.

### Three Plans — linear dependency chain

| Plan | Wave | Depends on | Files touched |
|---|---|---|---|
| `plan-01-vendor-api-types` | 1 | — | `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go`, `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`, CRD YAML |
| `plan-02-resolve-provider-vendor` | 2 | plan-01 | `internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`, `dispatch_helpers_test.go` |
| `plan-03-manager-helm-vendor` | 3 | plan-02 | `cmd/manager/env.go`, `env_test.go`, `charts/tide/values.yaml`, `deployment.yaml` |

**plan-01** adds the `Vendor string` field (with `+kubebuilder:validation:Enum=anthropic;openai` CEL marker) to `LevelConfig` and `SubagentConfig`, then regenerates deepcopy and CRD YAML via `make manifests generate`.

**plan-02** extends `ProviderDefaults` with `Vendor` and rewires `ResolveProvider` to walk the full precedence chain — `Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → helmDefaults.Vendor → "anthropic"` — with unit tests proving Codex-pinned levels resolve `"openai"` and Claude-pinned levels stay `"anthropic"` in the same Project.

**plan-03** threads the Helm vendor default through `cmd/manager/env.go` (`resolveDefaultVendor()`, extended `tideHelmProviderDefaults`) and wires `subagent.defaults.vendor` → `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR` env into `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml`, with `env_test.go` round-trip coverage. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` remain green throughout — zero OpenAI/Codex imports ever touch the controller.

spec.objective: Extend the v1alpha1 API types with a per-level Vendor field. Add Vendor string (with +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=anthropic;openai CEL marker) to both LevelConfig and SubagentConfig in api/v1alpha1/project_types.go, then run make manifests generate to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go and the CRD YAML under config/crd/bases/. No controller, chart, or cmd/ changes -- this plan establishes the schema foundation consumed by plan-02.

Rewire ResolveProvider in internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go to walk the full vendor precedence chain: Levels.<level>.Vendor -> Spec.Subagent.Vendor -> helmDefaults.Vendor -> fallback 'anthropic'. Extend ProviderDefaults with a Vendor string field so the Helm default can propagate. Add dispatch_helpers_test.go cases proving: (a) a Codex-pinned task level resolves Vendor:'openai'; (b) a Claude-pinned phase level resolves Vendor:'anthropic'; (c) a Project with no vendor override resolves Vendor:'anthropic' at every level -- all offline, no network calls. No chart or cmd/ changes in this plan.

Thread the per-level vendor default through cmd/manager/env.go and the Helm chart. Add resolveDefaultVendor() helper (reads TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR env var, fallback 'anthropic') and extend tideHelmProviderDefaults to populate ProviderDefaults.Vendor. Extend cmd/manager/env_test.go with a round-trip test: TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR=openai -> ProviderDefaults.Vendor=='openai'. Add subagent.defaults.vendor (and optional per-level vendor keys under subagent.levels.*) to charts/tide/values.yaml; wire TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR into charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml. Verify helm template renders the env under both default and --set override paths. make verify-import-firewall and make verify-dispatch-imports must remain green -- zero openai or codex imports in controller or cmd/manager.
